name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years diagnosing degradation and failure modes in functional polymer and thin-film systems through multi-technique characterization, DOE/SPC, and root-cause analysis. Translates lab evidence into reproducible test methods, SOPs, and cross-functional engineering recommendations for high-reliability materials.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided process-development experiments for bio-based polymer systems, linking process parameters to material quality, degradation behavior, and reproducible analytical specifications.
Use FT-IR, spectroscopy, thermal analysis, and statistical process-control concepts to investigate structure-property changes and recommend process/test-method mitigations.
Translate experimental findings into SOP-ready protocols, calibration expectations, and technical recommendations for repeatable execution across lab teams.

Designed and characterized 15+ electrochemical/thin-film material variants using cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, NMR, GPC, TGA, and DSC to diagnose structure-property-performance relationships.
Identified redox instability, oxidative degradation, and interface-related failure modes under electrochemical stress, then recommended material and process mitigations.
Built reproducible analytical datasets and technical reports supporting root-cause analysis, method comparison, and cross-functional research decisions.

Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications, developing surface preparation protocols and CVD-based deposition strategies to achieve controlled film morphology and device performance.

Developed and optimized CVD-based polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring, establishing process control parameters and characterizing film growth kinetics to improve coating reproducibility and uniformity.
Managed shared laboratory operations, instrument access, safety practices, and troubleshooting workflows for multidisciplinary materials-chemistry teams.
Improved documentation, training, and operating discipline for repeatable measurements and safer high-throughput experimental work.

projects.title: Electrochemical Materials Failure-Mode Analysis
projects.description: Diagnosed redox instability, oxidative degradation, and interface-related performance loss in functional electrochemical/thin-film materials, then translated findings into material and process mitigations.
projects.tech: Cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, DOE, degradation analysis

projects.title: Process-Controlled Polymer Systems
projects.description: Led DOE-guided process optimization for polymer systems, defining critical parameters, analytical readouts, and reproducible process specifications tied to material quality.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, FT-IR, thermal analysis, SOPs, root-cause documentation

projects.title: Thin-Film Coating Process Development
projects.description: Developed CVD/surface-preparation strategies for molecular coatings, translating process variables into film morphology and optical/material performance.
projects.tech: CVD, QCM-D, spectroscopy, surface preparation, process-structure-property analysis

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.

Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Failure & Reliability Analysis: Failure mode identification, root-cause analysis, degradation/stability testing, physics-of-failure mindset, FMEA principles, corrective-action recommendations, structured technical reports
skills.Materials Characterization: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, ellipsometry, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, TGA, DSC, NMR, GPC, QCM-D, GC-MS, LC-MS exposure; cross-technique interpretation of materials data
skills.Materials Systems: Organic/organometallic thin films, electrochromic materials, polymer systems, coatings, surface functionalization, process-structure-property relationships
skills.Data & Quality: DOE, SPC, JMP/basic, Python/basic, Excel, SOPs, calibration expectations, reproducibility studies, lab safety and operations management

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
